title: Method for forming a glass article
abstract: A method of redistributing cord along at least one surface of a glass ribbon drawn from a forming body in a fusion down draw glass making process. The method includes rolling the forming body about a longitudinal axis of the forming body and additionally, or alternatively, varying a pitch of the forming body.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of forming a glass ribbon, comprising: supplying molten glass to a forming body including a trough extending along at least a portion of a length L of the forming body, the trough including opposing first and second side walls, the molten glass filling the trough and overflowing the sidewalls, wherein molten glass overflowing the first side wall forms a first flow of molten glass and molten glass overflowing the second side wall forms a second flow of molten glass, the first and second flows of molten glass joining along a bottom edge of the forming body to form a ribbon of glass including a first layer and a second layer, respectively; and rotating the forming body about a longitudinal axis thereof through an angle a greater than zero degrees relative to a vertical plane parallel to the bottom edge and in a direction toward the second side wall such that a mass flow rate of the molten glass overflowing the second side wall is greater than a mass flow rate of the molten glass overflowing the first side wall such that a thickness of the second layer is greater than a thickness of the first layer. 2. The method according to claim 1 , wherein a is equal to or less than about 1 degree. 3. The method according to claim 1 , wherein a is in a range from about 0.3 degrees to about 0.4 degrees. 4. The method according to claim 1 , further comprising rotating the forming body about a lateral axis orthogonal to the longitudinal axis through an angle Î² relative to a horizontal plane. 5. The method according to claim 4, wherein a height of the bottom edge at an inlet end of the forming body is greater than a height of an end of the forming body opposite the inlet end, relative to a horizontal plane. 6. The method according to claim 1 , further comprising varying a viscosity of the molten glass overflowing the first and/or second side wall such that the viscosity of the molten glass 

 overflowing the first side wall is different than a viscosity of molten glass overflowing the second side wall. 7. The method according to claim 1 , wherein an angle of a floor of the trough in a direction orthogonal to the longitudinal axis, relative to a horizontal plane, is in a range from greater than 0 degrees and equal to or less than about 1 degree. 8. A method of forming a glass ribbon, comprising: supplying molten glass to a forming body including a longitudinal axis, and a trough extending along at least a portion of a length L of the forming body, the trough including opposing first and second side walls, the molten glass filling the trough and overflowing the first and second sidewalls; rotating the forming body about a lateral axis perpendicular to the longitudinal axis through an angle Î² relative to a horizontal plane; and wherein molten glass overflowing the first side wall forms a first flow of molten glass and molten glass overflowing the second side wall forms a second flow of molten glass, the first and second flows of molten glass joining along a bottom edge of the forming body to form a ribbon of glass. 9. The method according to claim 8, wherein a height of the bottom edge at an inlet end of the forming body is greater than a height of the bottom edge at an end of the forming body opposite the inlet end, relative to a horizontal plane. 10. The method according to claim 8, wherein the ribbon of glass comprises a first layer and a second layer, respectively, the method further comprising rotating the forming body about the longitudinal axis thereof through an angle a greater than zero degrees relative to a vertical plane parallel with the bottom edge and in a direction toward the second side wall such that a mass flow rate of the molten glass overflowing the second side wall is greater than a mass flow rate of the molten glass overflowing the first side wall, and wherein a thickness of the second layer is greater than a thickness of the first layer. 1 1. The method according to claim 10, wherein a is equal to or less than about 1 degree. 12. The method according to claim 1 1, wherein a is in a range from about 0.3 degrees to about 0.4 degrees. 13. The method according to claim 9, further comprising varying a viscosity of the molten glass overflowing the first and/or second side wall such that the viscosity of the molten glass overflowing the first side wall is different than a viscosity of molten glass overflowing the second side wall. 14. A method of forming a glass ribbon, comprising: supplying molten glass to a forming body including a trough extending along at least a portion of a length L of the forming body, the trough including opposing first and second side walls and a floor connecting the first and second side walls, the molten glass filling the trough and overflowing the first and second sidewalls, and wherein an angle of the floor of the trough in a direction orthogonal to the longitudinal axis relative to a horizontal plane is in a range from greater than 0 degrees and equal to or less than about 1 degree such that a mass flow rate of molten glass overflowing the second side wall is greater than a mass flow rate of molten glass overflowing the first side wall; and wherein molten glass overflowing the first side wall forms a first flow of molten glass and molten glass overflowing the second side wall forms a second flow of molten glass, the first and second flows of molten glass joining along a bottom edge of the forming body to form a ribbon of glass including a first layer and a second layer, respectively, and wherein a thickness of the second layer is greater than a thickness of the first layer. 15. The method according to claim 14, wherein a viscosity of the molten glass overflowing the first side wall is different than a viscosity of the molten glass overflowing the second side wall. 16. The method according to claim 15, wherein the viscosity of the molten glass overflowing the first side wall is greater than a viscosity of the molten glass overflowing the second side wall. 17. The method according to claim 15, further comprising heating and/or cooling the molten glass overflowing at least one of the first and second sidewalls. 18. The method according to claim 14, wherein a height of the bottom edge at an inlet end of the forming body is greater than a height of an end of the forming body opposite the inlet end relative to a horizontal plane positioned below the forming body. 19. The method according to claim 14, further comprising rotating the forming body about a longitudinal axis thereof through an angle a greater than zero degrees relative to a vertical plane parallel to the bottom edge and in a direction toward the second side wall such that a mass flow rate of the molten glass overflowing the second side wall is greater than a mass flow rate of the molten glass overflowing the first side wall. 20. The method according to claim 19, wherein angle a is greater than 0 degrees and equal to or less than about 1 degree.
